rationale: Empirical checks all pass. go build ./... clean; go test ./internal/validate/... and ./internal/doctor/... both ok (TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows PASS in isolation too). Grepped every domain's OWNERSHIP.yaml: only workflow claims any .claude/** path (skills, workflows) — no double-claim. Read isDocFile/isProcessArtifact (docsync.go:393, divergence.go:300): .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js hits none of their prefixes (docs/, .mindspec/, project-docs/, rootOperatorDocs, /reviews/, .beads/, mover-run-state, vestigial-config) — confirmed GOVERNABLE SOURCE per spec, matching the claim's rationale. Built the CLI and ran `mindspec doctor` against the live repo: all 4 domain manifests report [OK], no domain-overlap/redundant-subpath/duplicate-entry/dead-manifest warnings anywhere — the new claim introduces no drift. Traced GlobMatch by hand for '.claude/workflows/**': matches ms-panel.js (trailing-** segment logic) and correctly rejects a hypothetical '.claude/workflowsX/...' sibling (filepath.Match segment mismatch, no accidental prefix bleed). Test genuinely pins the claim (asserts owner=="workflow"; would fail without the added path).
findings: grep '.claude' across all .mindspec/domains/*/OWNERSHIP.yaml — only workflow domain claims .claude/** (skills, workflows); no overlap
mindspec doctor (built from this commit) against the live repo — all 4 domain OWNERSHIP.yaml manifests [OK], zero overlap/redundant/duplicate/dead-manifest warnings

isDocFile (docsync.go:393) and isProcessArtifact (divergence.go:300) both return false for .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js — confirms it is governable source, consistent with the bead's stated rationale
hand-traced GlobMatch('.claude/workflows/**', path): matches ms-panel.js; does not match a hypothetical '.claude/workflowsX/...' path (segment-level filepath.Match, no prefix bleed)
